metadata.fixture_purpose: Real production data sample for designing the Cultivo Pipeline / Batch Overview surface. Use this to size typography, sense distribution shapes, and texture the canvas with real strings (real batch codes, real strain names) before drawing the artboard. Every row is a real batch from production v_batch_lifecycle on 2026-04-26.
metadata.sanitization: v_batch_lifecycle does not expose customer names, order codes, or PII. allocated_orders/lines/units/revenue and shipped_g/sold_value are aggregated counts and totals only. No scrubbing required for this fixture. The deep-dive's allocations panel will eventually show order codes; those are sourced from a separate view and will need PII handling at that point. Not applicable here.

notes: 292 batches total; 134 inv_arm, 84 pg_arm (cultivation_only equivalent in v2), 74 skeleton_arm (orphan tier).
Of 102 closed batches, 52 are orphan-tier — historical archived rows with no production data. Default view hides orphan tier; count chip in header shows it.
Cultivation is dominated by cultivation_only confidence (74/87) because most living batches have plant_groups but not yet inventory_items.

Real-world sellable_now_g per batch ranges from 0 to ~2,300g. Real-world allocated_revenue per batch ranges from $0 to ~$12,000. Plan typography for four-to-five-digit grams and four-to-five-digit dollars per row.

Total grams in flight across all stages on this date: roughly 90,000g raw plus 8,000g sellable. Total gross projected revenue at the derived prices below: roughly $90,000 to $130,000 depending on conversion assumptions applied. Plan rollup typography for six-figure dollar totals.

edge_cases_included: 260127-ORS: orphan-tier with active packaged state, no production data — for designing the orphan empty state
260218-STP: closed batch with all sellable consumed (sellable_now_g=0) but realized revenue history
260218-GRM: cultivation veg-stage batch with mixed flower_plants and veg_plants counts

strain_wholesale_prices.doc: Per spec open question 1, Option C is the recommended source: manual override on strain row, fallback to 90-day rolling derived from sold_value / shipped_g. Below are the derived 90-day-rolling prices for strains in this fixture. Use these as the working price for any gross projected revenue calculation in mocks. For strains with no price history (no sold rows), use the manual override placeholder marked manual_override_required at the working assumption listed.

prices.note: no price history; recommend $3.00/g as the working flower-channel default
no price history; recommend $3.00/g working default
fresh-frozen, may settle below flower price; $3.00/g working default
no price history; recommend $3.00/g working default
bulk-channel-dominant strain; price reflects bulk economics, not packaged

conversion_factors_v1.doc: Working assumptions per spec section 4 conversion-yield discipline (brain row 0c221ef4). Use these to compute total_potential_g for batches in cultivation or drying when v_batch_lifecycle returns null for total_potential_g. Strain-specific outliers are real and respected.

strain_outliers.Animal Tsunami: smalls-only at bucking; 17.6% wet-to-dry; revenue ceiling at smalls pricing not flower
strain_outliers.Capulator Junky: highest flower yield (51%); fastest bucking; lowest waste
strain_outliers.Stay Puft: high flower yield at trim (66%); strong revenue per gram processed

stage_duration_assumptions_v1.doc: Working assumptions for expected stage durations (spec section 4). Used to compute days-to-cash and to flag operations-at-risk batches.

cost_to_actualize_factors_v1.doc: Working assumptions per spec section 4 cost-to-actualize cluster. Dollars per gross sellable gram, by channel, per remaining stage. Render in mocks as gross cost only — never net margin (per row 0c221ef4 discipline).

batches._persona_note: Cultivation, high confidence. Mid-cycle flower batch in FLW-10 with realistic next_harvest_date 18 days out. Position numbers populated because plant_groups exist and bucked_g already 1093g from sister batches' yield rollup pattern. Use this as the canonical 'healthy cultivation' row.

Cultivation, cultivation_only confidence, RUNNING LATE. 141 plants in flower at 68 days_in_stage with next_harvest_date 5 days in the past (2026-04-21). This is a textbook operations-at-risk row for the at-risk list — high plant count, large potential revenue, days_in_stage past expected_harvest. Use to design the running-late inline indicator.

Cultivation veg, cultivation_only confidence. 32 flower plants and 14 veg plants — a real mixed-stage batch. Use this to design how the row handles mixed plant counts and the lack of next_harvest_date in veg stage.

Drying, high confidence. 64.5kg wet binned to 11kg dry over 17 days — a healthy wet-to-dry conversion at 82.93% water loss. Use as the canonical drying-stage row. Real number magnitudes: total_wet_weight_g is five-digit (64,518), total_dry_weight_g is five-digit (11,015), batch_wet_to_dry_loss_pct is two-decimal precision.

Drying, none confidence. 67kg wet to 12.3kg dry in same 17 days — but confidence is 'none' because batch_id link to inventory_items is missing or strain attribution is incomplete. Use to design how confidence:'none' renders alongside high-confidence numbers — same shape, lower trust.

Processing_pending, cultivation_only, MASSIVELY STUCK. 108 days_in_stage in fresh_frozen state — should normally be 0-7 days. Textbook operations-at-risk + revenue-at-risk if this represents lost yield. Use for the at-risk list category badge design.

Processing, medium confidence, STUCK. 144 days in processing — way past expected 14-30 day window. 5928g binned with 2683.1g projected sellable. This batch's days_in_stage is the canonical 'severely stuck' indicator; should weight heavily in the at-risk attention sort. Quality grade C (lower).

Inventory, high confidence, partially allocated and partially shipped. 288.1g sellable_now (smalls only — Capulator Junky's signature pattern), $1,360 allocated across 2 orders, $12,640 already sold from 4,150g shipped. Use as the canonical 'live commerce' row in the deep-dive — every commerce-cluster field is populated.

Inventory, high confidence, UNALLOCATED. 2,272.5g sellable across 602g bulk_flower + 1,530.5g bulk_smalls + 140g packaged. Zero allocations. $1,120 already sold (224g shipped). This is the textbook 'sellable inventory at rest' row — Cult Cannabis has 199 lbs of bulk-ready material like this sitting unsold per revenue_stability_levers. Use to design grams-at-rest stage callout.
Inventory, medium confidence. Mid-tier confidence row — 592g sellable, $1,680 sold from 350g shipped. Use to design the medium-confidence sigil treatment alongside high-confidence rows.

Inventory, low confidence. 406.7g sellable across bulk_flower + bulk_smalls. $3,800 sold (1,120g shipped) so it has a realization history but current data is low-confidence — possibly batch_id attribution drift from the D17 phantom-row issue. Use to design the low-confidence sigil after the position number.

Inventory, ORPHAN tier. Lifecycle_state says 'packaged' but no production data — no bulk weights, no packaged grams, no allocations, no shipments. The kind of row that should be hidden by default with a count chip in the header. Use to design the orphan empty state when expanded via the confidence toggle.

Closed, high confidence, ACTIVE COMMERCE. Recently completed (2026-03-31) with full lifecycle weights captured: 35.5kg wet → 8kg dry → 2.56kg bucked → 462g sellable + 280g packaged. $8,480 allocated across 3 orders (6 lines, 266 units) and $18,260 already sold from 6,536g shipped. Use as the canonical 'closed and still being worked' row for the deep-dive timeline panel. Quality grade CULT (Cult Cannabis premium tier).

Closed, high confidence, FULLY DEPLETED. 30.9kg wet → 6.7kg dry → 0g sellable_now (all consumed). One order allocation $4,400 closing recently (5 days ago). $6,800 already realized from 3,080g shipped. Use as the 'just closed' row — the realization story is dominant, position is empty.

Closed, high confidence, BULK CHANNEL DOMINANT. 815.2g sellable across 45.2g bulk_flower + 770g bulk_smalls. No allocations currently but $13,280 already sold from 4,527g shipped — full realization history. Use to design how a bulk-channel-dominant batch reads in the deep-dive (cost-to-actualize was lower because no hand-trim labor). Quality grade CULT.
